ucs.title: [LLM] Thrip 'living-off-the-land' remote-access tooling install (LogMeIn / PsExec / WinSCP) on espionage-target hosts
ucs.description: Hunts the characteristic Thrip tradecraft documented by Symantec (2018): operators dropping legitimate dual-use admin/RAT utilities — LogMeIn, PsExec, WinSCP, Mimikatz, plus custom Catchamas/Mycicil/Spedear loaders — onto satellite-comms, telecom and defense-contractor endpoints to blend in with legitimate IT activity rather than relying on bespoke malware.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("logmein.exe","LogMeInIgnition.exe","LMI_Rescue.exe","PsExec.exe","PsExec64.exe","WinSCP.exe","winscp.com","mimikatz.exe","catchamas.exe","mycicil.exe") OR (Processes.process IN ("*\\logmein*","*\\winscp*","*PsExec*-accepteula*","*sekurlsa*","*lsadump*")) Processes.user!="*$" Processes.user!="SYSTEM" by host Processes.process_name Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name | where firstSeen > relative_time(now(),"-1d") | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer host [| tstats summariesonly=true count as histCount from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where earliest=-30d@d latest=-1d@d Processes.process_name IN ("logmein.exe","LogMeInIgnition.exe","PsExec.exe","WinSCP.exe","mimikatz.exe") by host | rename host as host] | where isnull(histCount) OR histCount=0 | sort - firstSeen
ucs.kql:
let BaselineDays = 30d;
let RecentHours = 24h;
let ThripTooling = dynamic([
    "logmein.exe","logmeinignition.exe","lmi_rescue.exe","ramaint.exe",
    "psexec.exe","psexec64.exe","paexec.exe",
    "winscp.exe","winscp.com",
    "mimikatz.exe","wce.exe",
    "catchamas.exe","mycicil.exe","spedear.exe"
]);
let ToolingHashesOrCmdlines = dynamic([
    "sekurlsa::logonpasswords","lsadump::sam","-accepteula",
    "/scp","/sftp","open sftp://","open scp://",
    "LogMeIn Hamachi"
]);
let Baseline =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(BaselineDays) .. ago(RecentHours))
    | where FileName in~ (ThripTooling)
         or ProcessCommandLine has_any (ToolingHashesOrCmdlines)
    | summarize by DeviceId, FileName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(RecentHours)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"            // skip machine accounts
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| where FileName in~ (ThripTooling)
     or ProcessCommandLine has_any (ToolingHashesOrCmdlines)
| join kind=leftanti Baseline on DeviceId, FileName     // host has never run this tool before
| extend ToolCategory = case(
    FileName has "logmein" or FileName has "lmi", "RemoteAccess-LogMeIn",
    FileName has "psexec" or FileName has "paexec", "LateralExec-PsExec",
    FileName has "winscp", "DataStaging-WinSCP",
    FileName has "mimikatz" or ProcessCommandLine has "sekurlsa", "CredAccess-Mimikatz",
    FileName has_any ("catchamas","mycicil","spedear"), "ThripCustomLoader",
    "Other-DualUse")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ToolCategory, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let BaselineDays = 30d;
let RecentHours = 24h;
let ThripTooling = dynamic([
    "logmein.exe","logmeinignition.exe","lmi_rescue.exe","ramaint.exe",
    "psexec.exe","psexec64.exe","paexec.exe",
    "winscp.exe","winscp.com",
    "mimikatz.exe","wce.exe",
    "catchamas.exe","mycicil.exe","spedear.exe"
]);
let ToolingCmdlines = dynamic([
    "sekurlsa::logonpasswords","lsadump::sam","-accepteula",
    "/scp","/sftp","open sftp://","open scp://"
]);
let Baseline =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(BaselineDays) .. ago(RecentHours))
    | where EventID == 4688
    | extend Bin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
    | where Bin in~ (ThripTooling) or CommandLine has_any (ToolingCmdlines)
    | summarize by Computer, Bin;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(RecentHours)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend Bin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
| where Bin in~ (ThripTooling) or CommandLine has_any (ToolingCmdlines)
| join kind=leftanti Baseline on Computer, Bin
| extend ToolCategory = case(
    Bin has "logmein" or Bin has "lmi", "RemoteAccess-LogMeIn",
    Bin has "psexec" or Bin has "paexec", "LateralExec-PsExec",
    Bin has "winscp", "DataStaging-WinSCP",
    Bin has "mimikatz" or CommandLine has "sekurlsa", "CredAccess-Mimikatz",
    Bin has_any ("catchamas","mycicil","spedear"), "ThripCustomLoader",
    "Other-DualUse")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ToolCategory, NewProcessName,
          CommandLine, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Thrip Living-off-the-Land Toolset First Use on Host
id: 7a6c4c0e-2b51-4f0a-b9e3-4d4a3c1f8b21
status: experimental
description: Detects first-time-on-host execution of legitimate dual-use admin tools (LogMeIn, PsExec, WinSCP, Mimikatz) characteristic of Thrip espionage operations against satcom/telecom/defense targets.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0076/
  - https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/blogs/threat-intelligence/thrip-hits-satellite-telecoms-defense-targets
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.t1219.002
  - attack.t1588.002
  - attack.g0076
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\logmein.exe'
      - '\LogMeInIgnition.exe'
      - '\LMI_Rescue.exe'
      - '\PsExec.exe'
      - '\PsExec64.exe'
      - '\paexec.exe'
      - '\WinSCP.exe'
      - '\winscp.com'
      - '\mimikatz.exe'
      - '\catchamas.exe'
      - '\mycicil.exe'
  selection_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'sekurlsa::logonpasswords'
      - 'lsadump::sam'
      - 'open sftp://'
      - 'open scp://'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: (selection_image or selection_cmd) and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Sanctioned IT staff using PsExec/WinSCP via change ticket — pair with baseline join in SIEM
level: high
ucs.rationale: Thrip is uniquely defined by its 'living-off-the-land' use of LogMeIn for persistent remote access, PsExec for lateral movement, WinSCP for staging, and Mimikatz for creds — alongside custom Catchamas/Mycicil/Spedear payloads. Symantec's 2018 disclosure highlighted that Thrip's operational fingerprint is precisely this dual-use stack on satcom/telecom/defense hosts. The anti-join against a 30-day

ucs.title: [LLM] Thrip PowerShell-driven exfiltration over non-C2 channel (FTP / SMB / SCP) following credential-access activity
ucs.description: Hunts the Thrip exfil chain (T1059.001 + T1048.003) — operators using PowerShell, certutil, ftp.exe, or WinSCP to push staged archives to external infrastructure over an unencrypted non-C2 protocol on telecom/satcom/defense hosts that just exhibited credential-access tooling.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","ftp.exe","tftp.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","winscp.com","winscp.exe")) AND (Processes.process IN ("*Net.WebClient*","*Invoke-WebRequest*","*Invoke-RestMethod*","*UploadFile*","*ftp://*","*put *","*-urlcache*","*-encode*","*Compress-Archive*","*7z*a*-p*","*scp *","*open ftp://*","*open sftp://*")) Processes.user!="*$" by host Processes.user Processes.process_name | where firstSeen > relative_time(now(),"-24h") | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count as exfilBytes from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where earliest=-24h@h All_Traffic.dest_port IN (21,22,23,69,445,80,443) NOT (All_Traffic.dest_category="internal" OR All_Traffic.dest_ip="10.0.0.0/8" OR All_Traffic.dest_ip="172.16.0.0/12" OR All_Traffic.dest_ip="192.168.0.0/16") All_Traffic.bytes_out>1048576 by All_Traffic.src as host All_Traffic.dest_ip All_Traffic.dest_port | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename src as host] | sort - exfilBytes
ucs.kql:
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let ExfilWindow = 30m;
// 1. PowerShell / LOLBin invocation with archival or upload semantics
let StagingAndUpload =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","ftp.exe","tftp.exe",
                          "certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","winscp.exe","winscp.com",
                          "curl.exe","wget.exe")
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (
        "Net.WebClient","Invoke-WebRequest","Invoke-RestMethod","UploadFile",
        "ftp://","sftp://","scp://","open ftp","open sftp",
        "-urlcache","-encode","Compress-Archive",
        "put ", " -T ", "7z a -p", "rar a -hp")
    | project StageTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              StageBin = FileName, StageCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              StageParent = InitiatingProcessFileName;
// 2. Same host showed Thrip credential-access / dual-use tool in the prior 24h
let CredAccessHosts =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where FileName in~ ("mimikatz.exe","wce.exe","psexec.exe","winscp.exe","logmein.exe")
         or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("sekurlsa::","lsadump::","vaultcli")
    | summarize CredToolFirst = min(Timestamp) by DeviceId;
// 3. Egress to public IP on classic non-C2 ports within 30 minutes of staging
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where RemotePort in (21, 22, 23, 69, 445)         // FTP / SSH-SFTP / Telnet / TFTP / SMB
| where ActionType in ("ConnectionSuccess","ConnectionAttempt")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~
    ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","ftp.exe","tftp.exe","winscp.exe","winscp.com",
     "certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe")
| join kind=inner StagingAndUpload on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (StageTime .. StageTime + ExfilWindow)
| join kind=inner CredAccessHosts on DeviceId       // require prior cred-access on host
| project StageTime, ExfilTime = Timestamp,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, StageTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          StageBin, StageCmd,
          ExfilProc = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ExfilCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl,
          CredToolFirst
| order by ExfilTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let ExfilWindow = 30m;
let StagingAndUpload =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend Bin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
    | where Bin in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","ftp.exe","tftp.exe",
                     "certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","winscp.exe","winscp.com",
                     "curl.exe","wget.exe")
    | where CommandLine has_any (
        "Net.WebClient","Invoke-WebRequest","Invoke-RestMethod","UploadFile",
        "ftp://","sftp://","scp://","open ftp","open sftp",
        "-urlcache","-encode","Compress-Archive",
        "put "," -T ","7z a -p","rar a -hp")
    | project StageTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, StageBin = Bin, StageCmd = CommandLine;
let CredAccessHosts =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | extend Bin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
    | where Bin in~ ("mimikatz.exe","wce.exe","psexec.exe","winscp.exe","logmein.exe")
         or CommandLine has_any ("sekurlsa::","lsadump::","vaultcli")
    | summarize by Computer;
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
| where ipv4_is_private(DstIpAddr) == false
| where DstPortNumber in (21, 22, 23, 69, 445)
| where ActingProcessName in~
    ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","ftp.exe","tftp.exe","winscp.exe","winscp.com",
     "certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe")
| join kind=inner StagingAndUpload on $left.DvcHostname == $right.Computer
| where TimeGenerated between (StageTime .. StageTime + ExfilWindow)
| join kind=inner CredAccessHosts on $left.DvcHostname == $right.Computer
| project StageTime, ExfilTime = TimeGenerated,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, StageTime),
          DvcHostname, Account, StageBin, StageCmd,
          ExfilProc = ProcessName, ExfilCmd = ActingProcessCommandLine,
          DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber, Url
| order by ExfilTime desc
ucs.rationale: Thrip's MITRE-mapped exfil pattern is specifically T1048.003 (unencrypted non-C2 protocol) paired with T1059.001 (PowerShell) — i.e. they push collected files out via FTP, SMB or SSH from compromised satcom/telecom workstations rather than standing up dedicated C2. The detection chains three actor-specific signals (PowerShell/WinSCP/ftp.exe staging → public-IP egress on FTP/SFTP/SMB ports → prior

_field_issues.message: `Compress` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Archive` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Public` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`ConnectionSuccess` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`ConnectionAttempt` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`StageTime` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`CredAccessHosts` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`StageBin` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`StageCmd` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`RemoteIP` is not a column of DeviceProcessEvents; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceLogonEvents, DeviceEvents, AlertEvidence, ASCDeviceEvents.
`RemotePort` is not a column of DeviceProcessEvents; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceLogonEvents, DeviceEvents, ASCDeviceEvents.
`RemoteUrl` is not a column of DeviceProcessEvents; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceEvents, AlertEvidence, ASCDeviceEvents.
`CredToolFirst` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `Compress` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent. Did you mean `Computer`?
`Archive` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`StageTime` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`CredAccessHosts` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`DvcHostname` is not a column of SecurityEvent; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImNetworkSession, ImAuthentication, ImWebSession, ImFileEvent, ImDnsActivity, ImRegistryEvent.
`StageBin` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`StageCmd` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`ActingProcessName` is not a column of SecurityEvent; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of SecurityEvent; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`DstIpAddr` is not a column of SecurityEvent; it does exist on ImNetworkSession, ImWebSession, ImDnsActivity.
`DstPortNumber` is not a column of SecurityEvent; it does exist on ImNetworkSession, ImWebSession.
`Url` is not a column of SecurityEvent; it does exist on EmailUrlInfo, UrlClickEvents, ThreatIntelligenceIndicator, ImNetworkSession, ImWebSession.
